question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEntityTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes setEntityTransactionPredicate() to be called with null after it was already set to a valid predicate, causing entityTransactionPredicate to be reset to REJECT_ALL and all subsequent addEntityId() calls to silently drop entity transactions for the remainder of that RecordItem's processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a non-hook contract call child transaction (contractNum != 365) during a network partition that drops all previous siblings, causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to start traversal from previous==null and immediately return null, leaving hookParent unset and causing the contract result to be attributed to the wrong parent in getContractRelatedParent()?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the entityTransactionBuilder to be shared across multiple concurrent addEntityId() calls without synchronization, since entityTransactionBuilder is @NonFinal and not thread-safe, causing the builder's entityId field to be overwritten by a concurrent call and producing EntityTransaction objects with incorrect entityId values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a RecordItem with previous set to a RecordItem from a completely different transaction family (different payer, different file), causing parseParent() to match on timestamp alone without verifying that the previous item belongs to the same logical transaction group, linking unrelated transactions as parent-child?
